text:
Excellent! The issues were created successfully with the correct format.

**Summary**: I opened **15 GitHub issues** in the 'acme/web' repository, one for each merged pull request that closes a Linear issue. Each issue title follows the format 'Verify: ' followed by the pull request's title.

I'll start by understanding the structure of the relevant tables and then proceed to open the GitHub issues.

First, let me examine the tables to understand their columns:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests' ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

text: Now let me check the github_issues table structure

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_issues' ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

text:
Now I need to find all merged pull requests that close a Linear issue. I'll query the github_pull_requests table and then open GitHub issues for each one.

Let me first check how many such pull requests exist:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM github_pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

text:
There are 15 merged pull requests that close Linear issues. Now I need to open GitHub issues for each of these. I'll use an INSERT statement to create all issues at once.

Let me preview the insert query first to make sure I understand what…(+16)

tool_calls.name: explain_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, state, body)
SELECT 'acme/web', 'Verify: ' || pr.title, 'open', ''
FROM github_pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.state = 'merged' AND pr.closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

text: The query looks good. It will read from github_pull_requests and insert into github_issues. Now let me execute it to create the GitHub issues:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, state, body)
SELECT 'acme/web', 'Verify: ' || pr.title, 'open', ''
FROM github_pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.state = 'merged' AND pr.closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

text: The insert fired successfully. Now I need to verify how many issues were opened. The message confirms the insert fired but doesn't give the count. Let me check how many GitHub issues were just created for the 'acme/web' repository with titl…(+32)

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as issues_opened FROM github_issues WHERE repo = 'acme/web' AND title LIKE 'Verify: %'"}

text:
Perfect! I opened **15 GitHub issues**. 

Let me also verify that the titles match the pull request titles by checking a few examples

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT title FROM github_issues WHERE repo = 'acme/web' AND title LIKE 'Verify: %' LIMIT 5"}

data: {"rows":[{"title":"Verify: Billing webhook retries drop events: guard against billing-webhooks"},{"title":"Verify: Search relevance regression: refactor search-relevance"},{"title":"Verify: Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: refactor ios18-crash"},{"title":"Verify: Onboarding funnel drop-off: add te…(+273)
